symptoms.title: Symptom Checker
symptoms.description: Predict possible conditions based on how you feel
symptoms.selectDesc: Check all symptoms you are currently experiencing

disease_desc.Common Cold: A viral infection of your nose and throat (upper respiratory tract). It's usually harmless.
disease_desc.Influenza (Flu): A viral infection that attacks your respiratory system — your nose, throat and lungs.
disease_desc.Food Poisoning: Illness caused by eating contaminated food. Infectious organisms or their toxins are common causes.
disease_desc.Gastroenteritis: An inflammation of the lining of the intestines caused by a virus, bacteria, or parasites.
disease_desc.Migraine: A headache that can cause severe throbbing pain or a pulsing sensation, usually on one side of the head.
disease_desc.Pneumonia: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. The air sacs may fill with fluid or pus.
disease_desc.Allergic Rhinitis: An allergic response causing itchy, watery eyes, sneezing, and other similar symptoms.
disease_desc.Dengue: A mosquito-borne viral disease occurring in tropical and subtropical areas.
disease_desc.COVID-19: A contagious disease caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2).
disease_desc.Asthma: A condition in which your airways narrow and swell and may produce extra mucus.
disease_desc.Hypertension (High Blood Pressure): A condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high.
disease_desc.Diabetes: A group of diseases that result in too much sugar in the blood (high blood glucose).
disease_desc.Anemia: A condition in which you lack enough healthy red blood cells to carry oxygen.
disease_desc.Heat Stroke: A condition caused by your body overheating, usually as a result of prolonged exposure to high temperatures.
disease_desc.Malaria: A disease caused by a parasite, transmitted through the bite of infected mosquitoes.
disease_desc.Tuberculosis: A serious infectious disease that mainly affects your lungs.
disease_desc.Thyroid Disorders: Conditions that affect the thyroid gland, which regulates the body's metabolic rate.
disease_desc.Arthritis: Inflammation of one or more joints, causing pain and stiffness.
disease_desc.UTI: An infection in any part of the urinary system, the kidneys, bladder, or urethra.
disease_desc.Bronchitis: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes, which carry air to and from your lungs.
disease_desc.Sinusitis: A condition in which the cavities around the nasal passages become inflamed.
disease_desc.Jaundice: Skin or eyes turning yellow due to high level of bilirubin.
disease_desc.Chickenpox: A highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy, blister-like rash.

medicineSafety.title: Medicine Safety
medicineSafety.description: Verify medicine authenticity and report concerns
medicineSafety.authenticityDesc: Verify if your medicine is likely genuine or potentially at risk. This analysis is based on our local medicine safety database and packaging standards.
medicineSafety.safetyInfo: Fake medicines can be dangerous to your health. Always buy from licensed pharmacists. If you suspect a medicine is fake, do not consume it and report it to your local drug regulatory authority.

settings.dataPrivacyDesc: Your health data is protected with row-level security. Only you can access your medical reports, medicines, and health records.

help.title: Help & Safety
help.description: Emergency contacts and important safety information
help.dataSecureDesc: All health data is encrypted using industry-standard protocols. Row-level security ensures only you can access your data. We never share your information without explicit consent.
help.mentalHealthDesc: If you're experiencing a mental health crisis, please reach out to a helpline. You can call iCall at 9152987821 or Vandrevala Foundation at 1860-2662-345.
help.medicalDisclaimerDesc: VitalWave AI provides health information for awareness only. It is NOT a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider.
